title: origanum hirtum subsp. sardoum
intro: Origanum hirtum subsp. sardoum, also known as Sardinian oregano, is a subspecies of the Origanum hirtum plant, native to the Mediterranean region. It is a perennial herb characterized by its woody stems, hairy leaves, and pinkish-purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Sardinian oregano can be prepared as a tea, essential oil, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
